1	//! Lua incremental tri-color mark-and-sweep garbage collector (Phase D).
2	//!
3	//! Ported from `src/lgc.c` (Lua 5.4.7, 1744 lines, 73 functions).
4	//!
5	//! This crate (`lua-gc`) is permitted to use `unsafe` (ceiling: 20 blocks per
6	//! `harness/unsafe-budgets.toml`). Every block carries `// SAFETY: ...`.
7	//!
8	//! # Algorithm overview
9	//!
10	//! Lua 5.4 uses a tri-color incremental mark-and-sweep collector with an
11	//! optional generational minor/major cycle strategy. The three colors are:
12	//! - **White** (two alternating bits): not yet visited; dead after a cycle if
13	//!   still white.
14	//! - **Gray**: visited but outgoing references not yet traced.
15	//! - **Black**: fully traced; invariant: a black object cannot point to a white one.
16	//!
17	//! The GC advances through the states in `GcState` order via `single_step`.
18	//!
19	//! # Circular-dependency note
20	//!
21	//! All public functions nominally take `&mut LuaState` (defined in `lua-vm`).
22	//! That creates: `lua-gc → lua-vm → lua-gc`. Resolution is deferred to Phase B
23	//! (introduce a `GcHost` trait in `lua-types`). Imports of `lua-vm` types are
24	//! commented out below; Phase B re-enables them.
25	//!
26	//! # Gray-list design deviation
27	//!
28	//! In C, each GC object has an intrusive `gclist` field linking it into a gray
29	//! list (`g->gray`, `g->grayagain`, etc.). `types.tsv` removes `gclist` from
30	//! individual types and replaces every intrusive list with a
31	//! `Vec<GcRef<dyn Collectable>>` on `GlobalState`. The sweep / mark functions
32	//! below are adapted accordingly; see `// PORT NOTE:` comments.
33	//!
34	//! C: `#define lgc_c` / `#define LUA_CORE`
35	
36	// TODO(port): lua-vm types cannot be imported until Phase B resolves the
37	// circular dep (lua-gc → lua-vm → lua-gc). Written speculatively; rustc will
38	// report "unresolved import" which is an expected Phase-A error.
39	// use lua_vm::state::{LuaState, GlobalState};
40	// use lua_vm::table::LuaTable;
41	// use lua_vm::func::{LuaClosure, UpVal};
42	// use lua_vm::object::LuaUserData;
43	// use lua_vm::proto::LuaProto;
44	// use lua_vm::string::LuaString;
45	
46	#[allow(unused_imports)]
47	use lua_types::error::LuaError;
48	#[allow(unused_imports)]
49	use lua_types::gc::GcRef;
50	#[allow(unused_imports)]
51	use lua_types::value::LuaValue;
52	
53	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
54	// Phase-B stub types
55	//
56	// `LuaState` and `GlobalState` properly live in `lua-vm`, which depends on
57	// `lua-gc` — a circular import. These placeholder structs let `lua-gc`
58	// compile in isolation. Phase B replaces them with `use lua_vm::state::...`
59	// once the `GcHost` trait in `lua-types` breaks the cycle.
60	//
61	// TODO(phase-b): needs lua_vm::state::{LuaState, GlobalState, StringTable}.
62	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	
64	/// Phase-B stub for `lua_vm::state::StringTable` (`stringtable` in C).
65	pub struct StringTable {
66	    pub nuse: usize,
67	    pub size: usize,
68	}
69	
70	/// Phase-B stub for `lua_vm::state::GlobalState` (`global_State` in C).
71	///
72	/// Field types are placeholders sufficient for `lua-gc` to type-check; the
73	/// real definitions land with Phase B's `GcHost` trait. Non-snake-case
74	/// field names match the C source one-for-one to keep this port faithful.
75	#[allow(non_snake_case)]
76	pub struct GlobalState {
77	    pub gcstate: u8,
78	    pub gckind: u8,
79	    pub gcemergency: bool,
80	    pub gcstp: u8,
81	    pub gcstopem: bool,
82	    pub currentwhite: u8,
83	    pub gcpause: u8,
84	    pub gcstepmul: u8,
85	    pub gcstepsize: u8,
86	    pub genmajormul: u8,
87	    pub genminormul: u8,
88	    pub GCdebt: isize,
89	    pub GCestimate: usize,
90	    pub lastatomic: usize,
91	    pub gray: GcObj,
92	    pub grayagain: GcObj,
93	    pub weak: GcObj,
94	    pub allweak: GcObj,
95	    pub ephemeron: GcObj,
96	    pub tobefnz: GcObj,
97	    pub allgc: GcObj,
98	    pub finobj: GcObj,
99	    pub fixedgc: GcObj,
100	    pub sweepgc: GcObjCursor,
101	    pub firstold1: GcObj,
102	    pub finobjold1: GcObj,
103	    pub finobjsur: GcObj,
104	    pub finobjrold: GcObj,
105	    pub survival: GcObj,
106	    pub old1: GcObj,
107	    pub reallyold: GcObj,
108	    pub strt: StringTable,
109	    total_bytes_stub: usize,
110	}
111	
112	impl GlobalState {
113	    /// Phase-B stub for `g->mainthread` (returns the GcObj head of the main thread).
114	    ///
115	    /// # Safety
116	    /// Placeholder returns null; callers under Phase B will receive a real pointer.
117	    pub unsafe fn mainthread_raw(&self) -> GcObj {
118	        std::ptr::null_mut()
119	    }
120	
121	    /// Phase-B stub for `gettotalbytes(g)` (C macro returning total live bytes).
122	    pub fn total_bytes(&self) -> usize {
123	        self.total_bytes_stub
124	    }
125	}
126	
127	/// Phase-B stub for `lua_vm::state::LuaState` (`lua_State` in C).
128	pub struct LuaState {
129	    g: GlobalState,
130	}
131	
132	impl LuaState {
133	    pub fn global(&self) -> &GlobalState {
134	        &self.g
135	    }
136	
137	    pub fn global_mut(&mut self) -> &mut GlobalState {
138	        &mut self.g
139	    }
140	
141	    /// Phase-B stub for `luaE_setdebt`.
142	    pub fn set_debt(&mut self, _debt: isize) {
143	        todo!("phase-b: needs lua_vm::state::LuaState::set_debt")
144	    }
145	
146	    /// Phase-B stub for accessing the running thread as a raw GcObj.
147	    ///
148	    /// # Safety
149	    /// Placeholder returns null; Phase B supplies the live thread pointer.
150	    pub unsafe fn current_thread_raw(&self) -> GcObj {
151	        std::ptr::null_mut()
152	    }
153	}
154	
155	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
156	// GC state machine constants  (lgc.h)
157	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
158	
159	/// C: `#define GCSpropagate 0`
160	pub const GCS_PROPAGATE: u8 = 0;
161	/// C: `#define GCSenteratomic 1`
162	pub const GCS_ENTER_ATOMIC: u8 = 1;
163	/// C: `#define GCSatomic 2`
164	pub const GCS_ATOMIC: u8 = 2;
165	/// C: `#define GCSswpallgc 3`
166	pub const GCS_SWP_ALLGC: u8 = 3;
167	/// C: `#define GCSswpfinobj 4`
168	pub const GCS_SWP_FINOBJ: u8 = 4;
169	/// C: `#define GCSswptobefnz 5`
170	pub const GCS_SWP_TOBEFNZ: u8 = 5;
171	/// C: `#define GCSswpend 6`
172	pub const GCS_SWP_END: u8 = 6;
173	/// C: `#define GCScallfin 7`
174	pub const GCS_CALLFIN: u8 = 7;
175	/// C: `#define GCSpause 8`
176	pub const GCS_PAUSE: u8 = 8;
177	
178	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
179	// GC color bit positions  (lgc.h)
180	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
181	
182	/// C: `#define WHITE0BIT 3`
183	pub const WHITE0_BIT: u8 = 3;
184	/// C: `#define WHITE1BIT 4`
185	pub const WHITE1_BIT: u8 = 4;
186	/// C: `#define BLACKBIT 5`
187	pub const BLACK_BIT: u8 = 5;
188	/// C: `#define FINALIZEDBIT 6`
189	pub const FINALIZED_BIT: u8 = 6;
190	
191	/// C: `#define WHITEBITS bit2mask(WHITE0BIT, WHITE1BIT)` = 0b00011000
192	pub const WHITE_BITS: u8 = (1 << WHITE0_BIT) | (1 << WHITE1_BIT);
193	
194	/// C: `#define maskcolors (bitmask(BLACKBIT) | WHITEBITS)`
195	const MASK_COLORS: u8 = (1u8 << BLACK_BIT) | WHITE_BITS;
196	
197	/// C: `#define maskgcbits (maskcolors | AGEBITS)`
198	const MASK_GC_BITS: u8 = MASK_COLORS | AGE_BITS;
199	
200	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
201	// Generational GC age constants  (lgc.h)
202	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
203	
204	/// C: `#define G_NEW 0` — created in the current cycle
205	pub const G_NEW: u8 = 0;
206	/// C: `#define G_SURVIVAL 1` — created in the previous cycle
207	pub const G_SURVIVAL: u8 = 1;
208	/// C: `#define G_OLD0 2` — promoted by a forward barrier in this cycle
209	pub const G_OLD0: u8 = 2;
210	/// C: `#define G_OLD1 3` — first full cycle as an old object
211	pub const G_OLD1: u8 = 3;
212	/// C: `#define G_OLD 4` — really old; skipped in minor collections
213	pub const G_OLD: u8 = 4;
214	/// C: `#define G_TOUCHED1 5` — old object touched this cycle
215	pub const G_TOUCHED1: u8 = 5;
216	/// C: `#define G_TOUCHED2 6` — old object touched in the previous cycle
217	pub const G_TOUCHED2: u8 = 6;
218	
219	/// C: `#define AGEBITS 7` — mask for the bottom 3 bits of `marked`
220	pub const AGE_BITS: u8 = 7; // 0b00000111
221	
222	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
223	// GC stop-flag constants  (lgc.h)
224	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
225	
226	/// C: `#define GCSTPUSR 1` — stopped by user (`collectgarbage("stop")`)
227	pub const GCSTPUSR: u8 = 1;
228	/// C: `#define GCSTPGC 2` — stopped by the GC itself (during finalization)
229	pub const GCSTPGC: u8 = 2;
230	/// C: `#define GCSTPCLS 4` — stopped while closing a Lua state
231	pub const GCSTPCLS: u8 = 4;
232	
233	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
234	// GC mode constants  (lstate.h)
235	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
236	
237	/// C: `KGC_INC` — incremental collection mode
238	pub const KGC_INC: u8 = 0;
239	/// C: `KGC_GEN` — generational collection mode
240	pub const KGC_GEN: u8 = 1;
241	
242	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
243	// GC step / tuning constants  (lgc.c, lgc.h)
244	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
245	
246	/// C: `#define GCSWEEPMAX 100`
247	const GC_SWEEP_MAX: i32 = 100;
248	
249	/// C: `#define GCFINMAX 10`
250	const GC_FIN_MAX: i32 = 10;

424	/// C: `static GCObject **getgclist(GCObject *o)`
425	///
426	/// Returns a pointer to the `gclist` field of `o`, which varies by type.
427	///
428	/// PORT NOTE: In C, each collectable type embeds a `gclist` field for the
429	/// gray list. `types.tsv` removes `gclist` from individual types; gray lists
430	/// become `Vec<GcRef<dyn Collectable>>` on `GlobalState`. Phase D must either
431	/// re-add the `gclist` field or keep the Vec approach. This function preserves
432	/// the C logic for Phase D; callers may use `push_to_gray` helpers instead.
433	///
434	/// # Safety
435	/// `o` must point to a valid, fully-initialized GC object of one of the listed
436	/// types.  The returned pointer aliases a field inside `*o`.
437	unsafe fn get_gc_list(o: GcObj) -> GcObjCursor {
438	    // C: switch (o->tt) { case LUA_VTABLE: return &gco2t(o)->gclist; ... }
439	    // TODO(port): Phase D must define the byte offset of `gclist` within each
440	    // concrete #[repr(C)] type and compute the pointer arithmetically.
441	    // The sizes and offsets will be fixed once concrete types embed GcHeader.
442	    match (*o).tt {
443	        LUA_VTABLE | LUA_VLCL | LUA_VCCL | LUA_VTHREAD | LUA_VPROTO | LUA_VUSERDATA => {
444	            // TODO(port): return &(concrete_type_cast(o)->gclist)
445	            // For now return null; Phase D will supply the correct offset.
446	            std::ptr::null_mut()
447	        }
448	        _ => {
449	            debug_assert!(false, "get_gc_list: unrecognized tt={}", (*o).tt);
450	            std::ptr::null_mut()
451	        }
452	    }
453	}

455	/// C: `static void linkgclist_(GCObject *o, GCObject **pnext, GCObject **list)`
456	///
457	/// Prepends `o` to `*list` through `pnext` (the field inside `o` that will
458	/// point to the former head), and paints `o` gray.
459	///
460	/// # Safety
461	/// `o`, `pnext`, and `list` must be non-null pointers with aligned, live data.
462	unsafe fn link_gc_list(o: GcObj, pnext: GcObjCursor, list: GcObjCursor) {
463	    // C: lua_assert(!isgray(o));
464	    debug_assert!(!is_gray_marked((*o).marked), "link_gc_list: already gray");
465	    // C: *pnext = *list;  *list = o;  set2gray(o);
466	    *pnext = *list;
467	    *list = o;
468	    (*o).marked = set_to_gray((*o).marked);
469	}

489	///
490	/// Returns `true` when GC object `o` should be cleared from a weak table.
491	/// `NULL` (non-collectable) → never cleared.
492	/// Strings → always marked (never cleared).
493	/// Other white objects → cleared (collected).
494	///
495	/// # Safety
496	/// `o` must be null or a pointer to a live `GcHeader`.
497	unsafe fn is_cleared(cur_white: u8, o: GcObj) -> bool {
498	    if o.is_null() {
499	        // C: if (o == NULL) return 0;
500	        return false;
501	    }
502	    // C: novariant(o->tt) == LUA_TSTRING
503	    // novariant strips the variant bits: (tt & 0x0F) gives the base type.
504	    // LUA_TSTRING base type is 4 (= 0x04 & 0x0F).
505	    let base_type = (*o).tt & 0x0F;
506	    if base_type == 0x04 {
507	        // C: markobject(g, o); return 0;  — strings are treated as values
508	        // markobject(g,t) = if (iswhite(t)) reallymarkobject(g, obj2gco(t))
509	        // We can't call really_mark_object here without &mut LuaState;
510	        // caller must ensure strings in weak tables are marked separately.
511	        // TODO(port): supply state parameter for the markobject call on strings.
512	        return false;
513	    }
514	    // C: else return iswhite(o);
515	    is_white_marked((*o).marked)
516	}

232	/// Type alias used throughout this module for GC object pointers.
233	///
234	/// C: `GCObject *`
235	///
236	/// In Phase D every collectable object begins with a `GcHeader`, so casting
237	/// between `*mut GcHeader` and `*mut ConcreteType` is sound when both are
238	/// `#[repr(C)]`.
239	pub type GcObj = *mut GcHeader;
240	
241	/// Type alias for a pointer-to-list-head, used for list cursor manipulation.
242	///
243	/// C: `GCObject **p` (pointer to the "prev-next" slot, enabling O(1) removal)
244	///
245	/// PORT NOTE: In Phase D this models the intrusive-list cursor pattern directly
246	/// as in C. Phase B/D must ensure that every `*mut GcObj` written through a
247	/// cursor keeps the list consistent.
248	pub type GcObjCursor = *mut GcObj;

250	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
251	// GC type-tag constants used in dispatch
252	// These mirror the `makevariant(LUA_T*, variant)` values from lobject.h.
253	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
254	
255	/// C: `LUA_VSHRSTR` = short interned string
256	const LUA_VSHRSTR: u8 = 0x04; // makevariant(LUA_TSTRING, 0)
257	/// C: `LUA_VLNGSTR` = long heap-allocated string
258	const LUA_VLNGSTR: u8 = 0x14; // makevariant(LUA_TSTRING, 1)
259	/// C: `LUA_VUPVAL`
260	const LUA_VUPVAL: u8 = 0x40; // makevariant(LUA_TUPVAL, 0)
261	/// C: `LUA_VUSERDATA`
262	const LUA_VUSERDATA: u8 = 0x08; // makevariant(LUA_TUSERDATA, 0)
263	/// C: `LUA_VLCL` = Lua closure
264	const LUA_VLCL: u8 = 0x46; // makevariant(LUA_TFUNCTION, 0)
265	/// C: `LUA_VCCL` = C closure
266	const LUA_VCCL: u8 = 0x56; // makevariant(LUA_TFUNCTION, 1)
267	/// C: `LUA_VTABLE`
268	const LUA_VTABLE: u8 = 0x05; // makevariant(LUA_TTABLE, 0)
269	/// C: `LUA_VTHREAD`
270	const LUA_VTHREAD: u8 = 0x09; // makevariant(LUA_TTHREAD, 0)
271	/// C: `LUA_VPROTO`
272	const LUA_VPROTO: u8 = 0x42; // makevariant(LUA_TPROTO, 0)
273	
274	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
275	// Inline bit-manipulation helpers (from lgc.h macros)
276	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
277	
278	/// C: `luaC_white(g)` — returns the current white color mask.
279	#[inline]
280	pub fn current_white(current_white: u8) -> u8 {
281	    // C: cast_byte((g)->currentwhite & WHITEBITS)
282	    current_white & WHITE_BITS
283	}
284	
285	/// C: `otherwhite(g)` — returns the OTHER white (used to detect dead objects).
286	#[inline]
287	pub fn other_white(current_white: u8) -> u8 {
288	    // C: (g)->currentwhite ^ WHITEBITS
289	    current_white ^ WHITE_BITS
290	}
291	
292	/// C: `iswhite(x)` — true if `marked` has either white bit set.
293	#[inline]
294	pub fn is_white_marked(marked: u8) -> bool {
295	    // C: testbits((x)->marked, WHITEBITS)
296	    (marked & WHITE_BITS) != 0

203	/// In C this is the `CommonHeader` macro, expanding to three fields at the
204	/// top of every `GCObject`-derived struct:
205	/// ```c
206	/// struct GCObject *next;   // next in allgc / finobj / fixedgc list
207	/// lu_byte tt;              // type tag
208	/// lu_byte marked;          // GC color + age bits
209	/// ```
210	///
211	/// In Rust (Phase D), every collectable type will carry one of these at
212	/// offset 0 (ensured by `#[repr(C)]`), so that `*mut GcHeader` casts are
213	/// sound.
214	///
215	/// Phase A-C: structure is defined here but not yet embedded in types (those
216	/// still use `Rc<T>`). Phase D replaces `GcRef<T>` with a raw-pointer arena.
217	///
218	/// TODO(port): Phase D must add `#[repr(C)] pub struct GcHeader { ... }` field
219	/// at the top of LuaString, LuaTable, LuaClosure, LuaProto, LuaUserData,
220	/// UpVal, and LuaState, and derive the `GcManaged` trait for each.
221	#[repr(C)]
222	pub struct GcHeader {
223	    /// Intrusive `next` pointer for allgc / finobj / fixedgc linked lists.
224	    /// NULL if this is the last element.
225	    pub next: *mut GcHeader,
226	    /// Internal type tag — same values as `LuaType` / variant tags.
227	    pub tt: u8,
228	    /// GC color bits (bits 3-5) and generational age (bits 0-2).
229	    pub marked: u8,
230	}
231

1	//! Lua incremental tri-color mark-and-sweep garbage collector (Phase D).
2	//!
3	//! Ported from `src/lgc.c` (Lua 5.4.7, 1744 lines, 73 functions).
4	//!
5	//! This crate (`lua-gc`) is permitted to use `unsafe` (ceiling: 20 blocks per
6	//! `harness/unsafe-budgets.toml`). Every block carries `// SAFETY: ...`.
7	//!
8	//! # Algorithm overview
9	//!
10	//! Lua 5.4 uses a tri-color incremental mark-and-sweep collector with an
11	//! optional generational minor/major cycle strategy. The three colors are:
12	//! - **White** (two alternating bits): not yet visited; dead after a cycle if
13	//!   still white.
14	//! - **Gray**: visited but outgoing references not yet traced.
15	//! - **Black**: fully traced; invariant: a black object cannot point to a white one.
16	//!
17	//! The GC advances through the states in `GcState` order via `single_step`.
18	//!
19	//! # Circular-dependency note
20	//!
21	//! All public functions nominally take `&mut LuaState` (defined in `lua-vm`).
22	//! That creates: `lua-gc → lua-vm → lua-gc`. Resolution is deferred to Phase B
23	//! (introduce a `GcHost` trait in `lua-types`). Imports of `lua-vm` types are
24	//! commented out below; Phase B re-enables them.
25	//!
26	//! # Gray-list design deviation
27	//!
28	//! In C, each GC object has an intrusive `gclist` field linking it into a gray
29	//! list (`g->gray`, `g->grayagain`, etc.). `types.tsv` removes `gclist` from
30	//! individual types and replaces every intrusive list with a
31	//! `Vec<GcRef<dyn Collectable>>` on `GlobalState`. The sweep / mark functions
32	//! below are adapted accordingly; see `// PORT NOTE:` comments.
33	//!
34	//! C: `#define lgc_c` / `#define LUA_CORE`
35	
36	// Canonical interpreter types live in `lua-vm` per harness/type-vocabulary.tsv.
37	// This crate re-exports them rather than defining local stubs.
38	//
39	// TODO_ARCH(phase-b-reconcile): a `GcHost` trait in `lua-types` should let
40	// `lua-gc` operate on a thinner abstraction than `LuaState`. Until that lands,
41	// we depend on `lua-vm` directly. `lua-vm` does NOT currently depend on
42	// `lua-gc`, so there is no real cycle.
43	
44	#[allow(unused_imports)]
45	use lua_types::error::LuaError;
46	#[allow(unused_imports)]
47	use lua_types::gc::GcRef;
48	#[allow(unused_imports)]
49	use lua_types::value::LuaValue;
50	
51	pub use lua_vm::state::{GlobalState, LuaState};
52	
53	/// Helper accessors that the GC needs but the canonical `LuaState` /
54	/// `GlobalState` do not yet expose. Implemented over the canonical types via
55	/// extension traits so we do not duplicate `LuaState`/`GlobalState`.
56	pub trait LuaStateGcExt {
57	    /// Phase-B stub for `luaE_setdebt`.
58	    fn set_debt(&mut self, debt: isize);
59	
60	    /// Phase-B stub for accessing the running thread as a raw GcObj.
61	    ///
62	    /// # Safety
63	    /// Returns null in Phase B; Phase D returns a live thread pointer.
64	    unsafe fn current_thread_raw(&self) -> GcObj;
65	}
66	
67	impl LuaStateGcExt for LuaState {
68	    fn set_debt(&mut self, _debt: isize) {
69	        todo!("phase-b-reconcile: lua_vm::state::LuaState::set_debt not implemented")
70	    }
71	    unsafe fn current_thread_raw(&self) -> GcObj {
72	        std::ptr::null_mut()
73	    }
74	}
75	
76	pub trait GlobalStateGcExt {
77	    /// Phase-B stub for `g->mainthread` (raw GcObj head of the main thread).
78	    ///
79	    /// # Safety
80	    /// Placeholder returns null; Phase D supplies a real pointer.
81	    unsafe fn mainthread_raw(&self) -> GcObj;
82	}
83	
84	impl GlobalStateGcExt for GlobalState {
85	    unsafe fn mainthread_raw(&self) -> GcObj {
86	        std::ptr::null_mut()
87	    }
88	}
89	
90	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
91	// GC state machine constants  (lgc.h)
92	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
93	
94	/// C: `#define GCSpropagate 0`
95	pub const GCS_PROPAGATE: u8 = 0;
96	/// C: `#define GCSenteratomic 1`
97	pub const GCS_ENTER_ATOMIC: u8 = 1;
98	/// C: `#define GCSatomic 2`
99	pub const GCS_ATOMIC: u8 = 2;
100	/// C: `#define GCSswpallgc 3`
101	pub const GCS_SWP_ALLGC: u8 = 3;
102	/// C: `#define GCSswpfinobj 4`
103	pub const GCS_SWP_FINOBJ: u8 = 4;
104	/// C: `#define GCSswptobefnz 5`
105	pub const GCS_SWP_TOBEFNZ: u8 = 5;
106	/// C: `#define GCSswpend 6`
107	pub const GCS_SWP_END: u8 = 6;
108	/// C: `#define GCScallfin 7`
109	pub const GCS_CALLFIN: u8 = 7;
110	/// C: `#define GCSpause 8`
111	pub const GCS_PAUSE: u8 = 8;
112	
113	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
114	// GC color bit positions  (lgc.h)
115	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
116	
117	/// C: `#define WHITE0BIT 3`
118	pub const WHITE0_BIT: u8 = 3;
119	/// C: `#define WHITE1BIT 4`
120	pub const WHITE1_BIT: u8 = 4;
121	/// C: `#define BLACKBIT 5`
122	pub const BLACK_BIT: u8 = 5;
123	/// C: `#define FINALIZEDBIT 6`
124	pub const FINALIZED_BIT: u8 = 6;
125	
126	/// C: `#define WHITEBITS bit2mask(WHITE0BIT, WHITE1BIT)` = 0b00011000
127	pub const WHITE_BITS: u8 = (1 << WHITE0_BIT) | (1 << WHITE1_BIT);
128	
129	/// C: `#define maskcolors (bitmask(BLACKBIT) | WHITEBITS)`
130	const MASK_COLORS: u8 = (1u8 << BLACK_BIT) | WHITE_BITS;
131	
132	/// C: `#define maskgcbits (maskcolors | AGEBITS)`
133	const MASK_GC_BITS: u8 = MASK_COLORS | AGE_BITS;
134	
135	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
136	// Generational GC age constants  (lgc.h)
137	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
138	
139	/// C: `#define G_NEW 0` — created in the current cycle
140	pub const G_NEW: u8 = 0;
141	/// C: `#define G_SURVIVAL 1` — created in the previous cycle
142	pub const G_SURVIVAL: u8 = 1;
143	/// C: `#define G_OLD0 2` — promoted by a forward barrier in this cycle
144	pub const G_OLD0: u8 = 2;
145	/// C: `#define G_OLD1 3` — first full cycle as an old object
146	pub const G_OLD1: u8 = 3;
147	/// C: `#define G_OLD 4` — really old; skipped in minor collections
148	pub const G_OLD: u8 = 4;
149	/// C: `#define G_TOUCHED1 5` — old object touched this cycle
150	pub const G_TOUCHED1: u8 = 5;
151	/// C: `#define G_TOUCHED2 6` — old object touched in the previous cycle
152	pub const G_TOUCHED2: u8 = 6;
153	
154	/// C: `#define AGEBITS 7` — mask for the bottom 3 bits of `marked`
155	pub const AGE_BITS: u8 = 7; // 0b00000111
156	
157	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
158	// GC stop-flag constants  (lgc.h)
159	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
160	
161	/// C: `#define GCSTPUSR 1` — stopped by user (`collectgarbage("stop")`)
162	pub const GCSTPUSR: u8 = 1;
163	/// C: `#define GCSTPGC 2` — stopped by the GC itself (during finalization)
164	pub const GCSTPGC: u8 = 2;
165	/// C: `#define GCSTPCLS 4` — stopped while closing a Lua state
166	pub const GCSTPCLS: u8 = 4;
167	
168	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
169	// GC mode constants  (lstate.h)
170	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
171	
172	/// C: `KGC_INC` — incremental collection mode
173	pub const KGC_INC: u8 = 0;
174	/// C: `KGC_GEN` — generational collection mode
175	pub const KGC_GEN: u8 = 1;
176	
177	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
178	// GC step / tuning constants  (lgc.c, lgc.h)
179	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
180	
181	/// C: `#define GCSWEEPMAX 100`
182	const GC_SWEEP_MAX: i32 = 100;
183	
184	/// C: `#define GCFINMAX 10`
185	const GC_FIN_MAX: i32 = 10;
186	
187	/// C: `#define GCFINALIZECOST 50`
188	const GC_FINALIZE_COST: usize = 50;
189	
190	/// C: `#define WORK2MEM sizeof(TValue)` — bytes per unit of traversal work.
191	/// `TValue` maps to `LuaValue` in Rust.
192	const WORK2MEM: usize = std::mem::size_of::<LuaValue>();
193	
194	/// C: `#define PAUSEADJ 100`
195	const PAUSE_ADJ: isize = 100;
196	
197	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
198	// GC object header  (lgc.h / lobject.h)
199	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
200

content.text:
## Reconcile report — `lua-gc`

**FAIL violations**
- Before: 4 (two struct definitions + two duplicate-name fan-outs for `LuaState`, `GlobalState`).
- After: 0.

**Cargo deps added** (`crates/lua-gc/Cargo.toml`)
- `lua-vm.workspace = true` — canonical owner of `LuaState` / `GlobalState`.
  No new circular dep: `lua-vm` does not currently depend on `lua-gc`.

**Swap shape**
- Deleted the local `StringTable`, `GlobalState`, and `LuaState` stubs in `gc.rs`.
- Added `pub use lua_vm::state::{GlobalState, LuaState};`.
- Kept the two helper methods (`set_debt`, `mainthread_raw`, `current_thread_raw`) by moving them onto extension traits `LuaStateGcExt` / `GlobalStateGcExt` so we don't redefine the canonical types.

**`todo!("phase-b-reconcile: …")` stubs introduced**: 75 (one per `lgc.c`-derived `unsafe fn` body that depended on the deleted shape).

**Top blockers I couldn't resolve in scope**
1. Canonical `GlobalState` represents `allgc / gray / grayagain / finobj / tobefnz / fixedgc / weak / allweak / ephemeron` as `Vec<GcRef<dyn Collectable>>`, but `lgc.c`'s port uses `*mut GcHeader` intrusive linked lists. Bridging the two is a Phase-D rewrite, not a signature touch-up — every traverse/sweep/mark body was stubbed.
2. The generational cohort cursors (`firstold1`, `survival`, `old1`, `reallyold`, `finobjold1`, `finobjsur`, `finobjrold`) and the `GCdebt`/`GCestimate` PascalCase names don't exist on the canonical struct (renamed to `gc_debt` / `gc_estimate`, cohorts deferred to Phase D per the comment in `state.rs`). The young-generation / atomic-to-gen functions therefore cannot be reconciled without first growing the canonical `GlobalState`, which is out of scope for this crate.

Constants, bit helpers, `GcHeader`/`GcObj` types, and the new extension traits are preserved unchanged. `cargo check -p lua-gc` now compiles with warnings only.
